headline: Claude Fable 5 dominated the month with its tier routing system drawing both adoption and a guardrail backlash.
body: Claude Fable 5 anchored the window across release coverage, agentic coding workflows, and monetization builds, with its tier routing system and guardrails the recurring focus, and a backlash over refused prompts and suspected model fallback. Apple's WWDC 2026 shift away from on-device AI toward an NVIDIA cloud-compute partnership marked a notable move in routing and compute infrastructure. On the technique side, breakouts clustered around inference and reinforcement methods including KV cache quantization, action prediction, Q-target, and Target Distribution Design, while tool calling, GRPO, and trajectory reuse weakened. New entrants such as router-prompt co-evolution and heterogeneous data streams point to continued churn in routing and training design.
